session_hint: the kafka messages skipped when consumer restarted
question: What did we conclude caused the Kafka consumer to skip three days of backlogged messages?
turns: Our Kafka consumer group 'order-processor' restarted after being down for three days of maintenance. On restart it jumped straight to the latest offset and skipped all backlogged messages entirely.

The consumer group had no committed offsets for this deployment environment because we had wiped the consumer group metadata as part of a staging cleanup script that accidentally ran against production.

Root cause: with no committed offsets, Kafka fell back to the auto.offset.reset config, which was set to 'latest' in production. So the brand-new group (from Kafka's perspective) started consuming from the tip of the log and skipped three days of accumulated messages. Fix: set auto.offset.reset=earliest for backlog-tolerant consumers, and restore the correct committed offsets from the last known checkpoint before restarting.
